title: National Science Board eviscerated; Trump admin fires all 22 members
description: Members had planned to release report that US is ceding scientific ground to China.

title: Next El Niño could be tipping point for a hotter climate
description: Pacific heat pulse is temporary, but scientists warn that its climate impacts are not.

title: Gateway manufacturer finally acknowledges issue, fails to mention "corrosion"
description: "At this time, further comments would be premature."

title: Meet the players who lost big money on Peter Molyneux’s failed Legacy
description: After millions in NFT sales, the hyped “play to earn” game was effectively dead in weeks.

title: Strange New Worlds S4 teaser strikes a more serious tone
description: "I have ever been prone to seek adventure and to investigate where wiser men would have left well enough alone."

title: Prime Video drops full trailer for Spider-Noir
description: It's "a detective story, but the detective happens to also have spider powers”—EP Chris Miller.

title: New robotic control software avoids jamming their joints
description: Software lets robots learn from each other even if they have different hardware.

title: Artemis II broke Fred Haise's distance record, but he is happy to pass it on
description: "It wasn't a big deal. It just coincided with the fact that Moon was farther away from the Earth."

title: Palantir employees are talking about company's "descent into fascism"
description: Slack messages, interviews with current and former works paint picture of company in turmoil.

title: This is who's developing Golden Dome's orbital interceptors—if they're ever built
description: "If boost-phase intercept from space is not affordable and scalable, we will not produce it."

title: Google will invest as much as $40 billion in Anthropic
description: This follows a similar, but smaller, investment by Amazon just days ago.

title: Europe—not US—first to authorize Moderna's combo mRNA flu-COVID vaccine
description: Amid RFK Jr.'s anti-vaccine agenda, Moderna withdrew its FDA application last year.

title: FCC: Router ban includes portable hotspots, but not phones with hotspot features
description: FCC defines consumer routers expansively, updates FAQ to include Wi-Fi hotspots.

title: Why are top university websites serving porn? It comes down to shoddy housekeeping.
description: Hundreds of subdomains from dozens of universities have been hijacked by scammers.

title: In rare chickenpox case, itchy blisters mushroom into large, rubbery nodules
description: Treatment options are tricky. The teen opted to live with the masses.

title: Soldier won $410K in Polymarket bets on timing of Maduro capture, US alleges
description: It's like "Pete Rose betting on his own team," Trump says of arrested soldier.

title: Meet the 19-meter Cretaceous kraken that swam with mosasaurs
description: Layer by layer, researchers revealed the jaws of an ancient predator.

title: Report: Samsung execs worried company could lose money on smartphones for the first time
description: The AI-driven memory shortage is hitting Samsung's bottom line.

title: Man faces 5 years in prison for using AI to fake sighting of runaway wolf
description: Beloved wolf gripped the nation after burrowing out of the zoo.

title: Well, this is embarrassing: The Lunar Gateway's primary modules are corroded
description: "Preliminary findings indicate that the issue likely results from a combination of factors."

title: As electric aspirations fade, Porsche sells its stake in Bugatti
description: Porsche's stake in Bugatti and Rimac Group have been sold to private equity.

title: Six things I'll remember when I think about Tim Cook's version of Apple
description: Under Cook, Apple became hugely successful, if not always surprising.

title: Trump administration attempt to gut Endangered Species Act hits roadblock
description: House vote to defang the Endangered Species Act was unexpectedly cancelled
